Why these 4 processes are not 1 — design rationale per layer
each row pairs with the same-color box on the left; merging adjacent layers loses the listed property
virt-launcher-monitor → needs to be PID 1
• Pod has exactly one PID 1 in its namespace; whoever owns it must reap children
• if virt-launcher itself were PID 1, a panic / segfault leaves orphan libvirtd + qemu zombies
• monitor is intentionally minimal: small attack surface, hard to crash, never updates state
• clean Pod-level termination semantics → kubelet sees Pod exit → virt-controller recreates Pod
• merging into virt-launcher: lose crash containment; merging into libvirtd: would need C-side k8s logic
virt-launcher (Go) → KubeVirt-native API surface, separate from libvirt
• libvirt is C; KubeVirt code is Go → FFI in the same process is painful and hurts iteration speed
• KubeVirt-shaped RPCs (SyncVMI / FreezeVMI / hot-plug Disk …) operate on VMI types, not domain XML
• cmd-server gRPC is the ONLY surface virt-handler talks to → libvirt API can change without breaking handler
• converter, container-disk, ephemeral-disk, virtwrap.DomainManager all live here in one Go binary
• merging into libvirtd: would fork upstream libvirt (anti-goal); k8s/Go ecosystem alignment lost
libvirtd / virtqemud → reuse industry-standard VM management layer
• libvirt is the de-facto Linux VM API: domain XML, QMP fluency, snapshots, migration FSM …
• KubeVirt deliberately reuses it instead of re-implementing direct qemu QMP from Go
• live migration uses libvirt's MigrateToURI3 + AUTO_CONVERGE / POSTCOPY built-in mechanics
• same daemon used by oVirt, OpenStack, virsh → battle-tested, tracks qemu releases for free
• merging into qemu-kvm: qemu is per-VM, libvirtd manages the lifecycle around qemu → different lifetimes
qemu-kvm → pure hypervisor, one process per guest VM
• qemu's job is instruction / device emulation + KVM ioctl → not lifecycle, not k8s, not RPC
• one qemu process == one VM: cgroup limits, seccomp profile, OOM scope all naturally per-VM
• QMP is qemu's stable management protocol; libvirtd is its standard consumer
• vCPU = pthread; if guest panics, it's contained inside this process (and inside Pod cgroup)
• merging anything into qemu = bloating the most security-sensitive component → anti-pattern
Failure recovery: why these layers + PID 1 watchdog give clean error semantics
qemu crashes
SIGSEGV / OOM
libvirtd
domain SHUTOFF
virt-launcher
notify event up
virt-handler reconcile
VMI.status = Failed
• if virt-launcher dies (panic / OOM): monitor (PID 1) sees child exit → monitor exits → Pod terminates clean
• virt-controller observes Pod gone → recreates virt-launcher Pod for the same VMI → full re-init from VMI spec
• no half-state: never have libvirtd alive without virt-launcher to drive it; never zombie qemu without libvirtd
• restart granularity = whole Pod; KubeVirt does not try to surgically restart libvirtd or qemu in place
fork() + execve (PID parent-child)
cmd-server gRPC / notify-client (host ↔ Pod via UDS)
libvirt RPC / QMP (intra-Pod)
crash propagation (qemu → handler)
Source: kubevirt v1.8.2 cmd/virt-launcher-monitor, cmd/virt-launcher, pkg/virt-launcher/virtwrap, pkg/virt-handler
